title: Experts warn of oil oversupply and predict a turnaround in prices
summary: Morgan Stanley warns of possible market oversaturation, Shell forecasts a 65% increase in LNG demand by 2050, and the Iranian crisis tightens energy supply.

title: US Supreme Court blocks firing of Fed Governor Lisa Cook
summary: The court rules that President Trump cannot remove the Federal Reserve member, which reinforces the central bank's independence.

title: Yen falls to 40-year low against dollar and Japan considers intervention
summary: The Japanese currency weakens due to interest rate differentials and dollar strength, while Japanese authorities warn of possible currency intervention.

title: Asian stock markets close mixed with Hang Seng under pressure and yen at lows
summary: Asian stocks show mixed performance: Nikkei rises, Kospi holds steady, and Hang Seng suffers from extreme short positions, in a record quarter for the region.

title: Wall Street closes higher driven by tech rebound
summary: US indexes rise, boosted by the recovery of technology stocks, with the Dow Jones, S&P 500 and Nasdaq in the green.

title: Dax rises more than 200 points while Ibex remains flat awaiting news
summary: The German stock market leads gains in Europe, while the Spanish Ibex trades without significant changes, pending negotiations between the US and Iran.

title: Bitcoin falls below $60,000 and Strategy announces $2 billion share buyback
summary: Pressure from rising rates and Bitcoin sales by Strategy cause a fall in the cryptoasset, which the company tries to counteract with a buyback plan.

title: AeroVironment jumps 20% after posting solid quarterly results
summary: The military drone company exceeds revenue and profit expectations, boosting its stock and attracting analyst interest.

title: Chinese industry expands and Meituan presents AI model with domestic chips
summary: Chinese manufacturing activity grows, Meituan trains its new artificial intelligence model with domestic semiconductors, and Taiwan investigates smuggling of AI hardware.

title: SpaceX enters indexes and Rocket Lab acquires Iridium amid space sector consolidation
summary: SpaceX joins the main stock indexes with a 16% rise, while Rocket Lab closes a historic deal to buy Iridium, consolidating the sector.

title: Nearly one million new millionaires in 2025, according to Global Wealth Report
summary: The number of millionaires in the world continues to grow, with Germany hosting 2.6 million people with high net worth.

title: Warren Buffett pauses his donations to the Gates Foundation over Epstein case
summary: The investor suspends multi-million dollar transfers to the foundation while Epstein's links to the organization are investigated.

title: Wall Street divided between buying Palantir and selling SanDisk
summary: Analysts recommend opposite positions for the two tech companies, betting on one and advising against the other.

title: Historical patterns anticipate the next move of the cryptocurrency market
summary: An analysis based on past cycles suggests what the next step for the crypto market will be.

title: Can $10,000 in Berkshire Hathaway make you a millionaire?
summary: A theoretical calculation shows the growth potential of Berkshire Hathaway shares over the long term.

title: Three factors that could boost international stocks for years
summary: Attractive valuation, global economic growth, and geographic diversification are the catalysts that favor markets outside the US.

title: Axcelis doubles in the year: is it time to buy?
summary: Shares of the semiconductor equipment maker have risen 100% in 2025, and analysts evaluate its future potential.

title: Ouster jumps 28% and hits all-time highs after deal with AIM
summary: The LiDAR sensor company benefits from robotics demand and a major deal, pushing its shares to record levels.

title: French inflation falls to 2%, German unemployment stable and UK GDP accelerates 0.6%
summary: European macroeconomic data show price moderation in France, an unchanged German labor market and sustained growth in the United Kingdom.

title: UK regulator wants to open app stores to competition and softens crypto rules
summary: The British competition authority proposes measures to foster rivalry in Apple and Google's app stores, while at the same time relaxing regulations on cryptocurrencies.
